steps.title: Identify the decisive finding
steps.content: The passage explains that variation within a lake undermines sampling reliability. Zhao et al. found no significant variation in U-238 and U-234/U-238 AR within their two lakes.

steps.title: Match the implication to the correct choice
steps.content: C correctly interprets what this finding implies: if values are uniform across depths and locations, any sample is representative of the entire lake, so localized sampling is reliable. The other choices either misidentify what variation was studied, or overclaim beyond what the data supports. That matches choice C — C is correct.
